answer:
**Andreas** (Cynewulf): This Old English poem features direct speech from God/Jesus to the apostle Matthew in prison ("Hold fast, my Matthew! Do not lose heart. I shall not leave you lost in this torment..."),[^1] and to Andrew, commanding his mission ("You must go now... to the land of the man-eaters..."). Jesus appears visibly and converses extensively with Andrew during his trials.[^2]

**Elene** (Cynewulf): An angel speaks to Constantine in a vision ("Constantine, king! Cast out your terror... By this sign, conquer"), instructing him on the True Cross.[^3] Judas prays aloud to God, who responds with a miraculous sign (rising smoke); the devil also debates Judas.[^4]

**Juliana** (Cynewulf): A devil disguised as an angel tempts Juliana ("What do you suffer... Be quick, when he orders you brought out..."); God replies from the clouds ("Seize the pretender and hold him fast"). Juliana interrogates and rebukes the devil at length.[^5]

**Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana** (Mpu Dusun): Buddha instructs the demon ascetic Kuñjarakarṇa on dharma ("My son, how fortunate... Go and reach the Cauldron of the god Yama"); Yama explains karma and hell to him ("That, man, is a precious birth..."). Extensive back-and-forth.[^6]

**Vrhaspatitattva**: The god Īśvara (Śiva) teaches the sage Vṛhaspati cosmology, tattvas, and yoga in long dialogues ("How exceedingly divine is your question... The supreme *tattva* is twofold"), refuting his doubts verse-by-verse.[^7]

No other works in the corpus contain explicit quoted dialogue between divine beings (God, angels, devils, Yama, Buddha, Śiva) and humans/ascetics. Philosophical texts like smṛtis or economic treatises lack it; poems like elegies have no such exchanges.

[^1]: *Andreas*, ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-bdbda7).
[^2]: *Andreas*, ch. 1, [chapter](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/).
[^3]: *Elene*, ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-306e06).
[^4]: *Elene*, ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1).
[^5]: *Juliana*, ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-a370d7).
[^6]: *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, ch. 1, [chapter](/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/).
[^7]: *Vrhaspatitattva*, ch. 1, [chapter](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/).
